name: loop-kit-dev
description: [DEV] Turn-gated loop plugin that keeps one turn alive until explicit user stop and applies phase protocols from turn-gate.

interface.shortDescription: [DEV] Turn-gated continuity with execution, refinement, review, and readiness phase protocols driven from one main surface.
interface.longDescription: [DEV] Provides a narrow operational plugin where turn-gate is the main user-facing surface. It keeps one turn alive until the user explicitly ends the work, runs each flow through preparation, work, verification, reporting, and next-flow reopening, records initial preparation and planned-flow design as an operational-preparation flow when it owns session plan, flow-list, scope, or approval-boundary artifacts, treats execution planned flows as cohesive reviewable or commit-sized change units rather than phase checklists, and chooses verification method as clean-context, normal, or not-required based on work risk. Self-drive is a separate overlay reference for prepared autonomous sequences.
defaultPrompt: Keep this turn alive until I explicitly stop; after reporting, reopen next-flow with explicit choices unless closure is source-recorded.
Run each turn-gated flow through preparation, work, verification, reporting, and next-flow reopening.
Treat planned flows as cohesive reviewable or commit-sized change units, not analysis/work/verification phase slices and not only direct user-visible value.
Classify verification method as clean-context, normal, or not-required while keeping result status separate as pass, fail, blocked, or insufficient.
Use clean-context verifier by default for file changes, release surfaces, multi-file contracts, failed checks, user-requested verification, and approval-sensitive actions.
Use normal evidence checks only for lower-risk no-edit/read-only work, and use not-required only when there is no work output to verify; always record reason and residual uncertainty.
Never infer approval for destructive, external, commit, push, PR, publish, release, or version bump actions from readiness, self-drive, verification method, or prior context.
Use turn-gate as the main controller, choose the current phase protocol as needed, and keep self-drive as a separate overlay for explicitly prepared autonomous sequences.
